w:r.w:t: # Launch Readiness Console – Product Requirements Document (PRD)

ClearCompany runs multiple product launches and feature releases in parallel across ~15 products/pods. Today, launch readiness is managed via a spreadsheet-based “Go / Conditional Go / No Go” matrix per epic, filled in by various stakeholders and manually reviewed in GTM/roadmap meetings.
- It’s easy for criteria to be stale or incomplete.
- Launch risks are only discovered late, often within weeks of target dates.
- There’s no single view across all launches; everything is buried in Sheets and slides.
- Nobody gets systematic reminders or alerts; everything is ad hoc.
**Launch Readiness Console** is an internal tool to replace the static spreadsheet with a structured, intelligent “control tower” for launches. It will:
- Model the existing matrix (criteria, stakeholders, Go/Conditional/No Go) in a real database.
- Automatically calculate launch readiness, highlight risks, and enforce gating rules.
- Notify stakeholders to update their pieces and summarize status for leadership.
- Integrate with Aha! so product and GTM data stay in sync.
This is a **single-tenant internal web app** used by ClearCompany’s product and GTM org.
We need to manage launch readiness across many simultaneous launches without relying on manual spreadsheet policing.
1. No consistent, real-time view of launch readiness across products.
2. Gating criteria (e.g., security, support readiness, migration) are often discovered too late.
3. Stakeholders forget to update statuses; the matrix gets stale.
4. Launch decisions (why we went Go/Conditional/No Go) aren’t logged cleanly.
5. Aha! is the roadmap source of truth, but launch readiness lives outside it.
- Provide a **single, living view** of launch readiness across all active launches.
- Enforce consistent **Go / Conditional Go / No Go** logic with **gating criteria** and **tier-specific rules**.
- Make risks obvious early via **alerts, reminders, and simple scoring**.
- Integrate with **Aha!** to pull launchable epics and push back summary status.
- Support **T-based milestones** (e.g., T-90, T-30, T+30, T+90) and flag misalignment.
- Serve as the canonical tool for **Go/No-Go decision meetings** and **post-launch reviews**.
- No complex workflow engine beyond simple reminders & basic rules.
- No deep analytics dashboards yet (beyond readiness % and basic KPI fields).
- No dynamic AI agent automation in v1 (LLM-based summaries can come later).
- Needs a single view of major launches, risks, and readiness.
- Uses it in quarterly / monthly reviews and GTM councils.
2. **Senior Director of Product / Product Leadership (Dan + pod leads)**
- Wants to know which launches are at risk and why.
- Uses the tool to prioritize work and adjust roadmap.
- Own launch readiness for their epics/themes.
- Coordinate with PMM, Eng, Support to fill the matrix.
- Need a clear checklist and visibility into blockers.
- Responsible for GTM criteria (messaging, sales assets, campaigns).
- Need reminders and a clear view of their outstanding tasks.
- Own specific criteria (e.g., migration tooling, support readiness, docs).
- Need an easy way to see and update what they’re on the hook for.
- Owns configuration of criteria, tiers, gates.
- Runs Go/No-Go sessions and curates views for leadership.

Each launch (usually an Aha epic/theme) is represented as a set of **criteria rows**. For each criterion, a **Decision Owner** sets the status: **Go**, **Conditional Go**, or **No Go**, plus any conditions and due dates.
- Computes **launch-level readiness** and **verdict** (Go / Conditional / No Go).
- Highlights **gate criteria** that block launches if not met.
- Flags **time-based risk** (e.g., 30 days to launch but multiple gates not ready).
- Sends **reminders** to owners to update their items.
- Produces **portfolio and launch-level views** for leadership.
Auth: SSO via corporate IdP or Google Workspace.
Core object representing a launchable epic/theme. Maps 1:1 to an **Aha Epic**.
Global template definition of a readiness criterion (row).
Instance of a Criterion for a specific Launch.
Snapshot of launch readiness at a specific decision time (e.g., Go/No-Go).
- Required: name, product, tier, target date, owner.
- On creation, auto-attach active Criteria matching tier and create LaunchCriterionStatus rows with `NOT_SET`.
- PM/Product Ops can edit tier, target date, owner, notes.
- Recompute risk and reminders on change.
- Product Ops can create/edit global Criteria.
- Criteria grouped by category; `sort_order` controls display.
- `is_active = false` stops criteria being instantiated for new launches.
- Decision Owner or Product Ops sets `status` and updates notes.
- Base: `IN_EVALUATION` if any criterion is set.
- Else any gate == `CONDITIONAL_GO` with unresolved pre-launch condition → `CONDITIONAL_GO`.
- Tier 3: readiness ≥ 0.7 → `GO`, else `CONDITIONAL_GO` (must-have gates cannot be `NO_GO`).
Manual override allowed for Product Ops/CPO with a recorded reason.
- `HIGH` if `days_to_launch <= 30` and any gate not GO or readiness below threshold.
- `MEDIUM` if `days_to_launch <= 60` and readiness moderately below threshold.
- Name, Product/Pillar/Pod, Tier, target date, days to launch, status, readiness %, risk, gate counts.
- Filters: tier, pillar, pod, owner, status, timeframe.
- Header: launch name, product, tier, dates, status, risk, readiness bar, gate counts.
- Matrix: criteria grouped by category with status, owners, conditions, timestamps.
- All LaunchCriterionStatus where they’re decision_owner or condition_owner.
- Show launch, product, criterion, status, condition, due date, overdue flag.
- Decision date/time, type, verdict, notes, creator.
- Selecting snapshot shows read-only matrix state at that time.
- If `last_updated_at` older than X days (e.g., 14) and status != GO:
- Notify launch owner, pod lead, Product Ops.
- Top N launches by tier, risk, and days-to-launch.
- Include status, readiness, key gate blockers.
Channels: email in v1, Slack later.
- Aha Epics are the canonical **Launch** source.
- Read tier, GA date, owner, product/pod, priority, tags, etc.
- Write back readiness status, score, risk, Go/No-Go date, and a URL back to the console.
- A separate technical doc specifies exact field mapping and API details.
- All authenticated users: read access to portfolio and launch detail.
- Launch owner, Product Ops, CPO: edit launch metadata.
- LaunchCriterionStatus: editable by decision_owner and Product Ops; others read-only.
- Criteria configuration: Product Ops & CPO only.
- Store audit entries with timestamp, user, entity type/id, JSON diff.
- Launches in console vs Aha epics tagged as Launch.
- % of Tier 1/2 launches using console for Go/No-Go.
- % launches with all gate criteria updated in last 14 days.
- Count of Tier 1 launches slipping post-Go due to readiness issues.
- Portfolio Dashboard, Launch Detail, My Items.
